event: DoD C-UAS budget exceeds combined Group 1/2 UAS procurement by Q4 2026 — Joint Interagency Task Force 401 committed $600M+ to Operation Epic Fury counter-drone defenses (per DefenseScoop Apr 10), while DDP Phase 1 targets 30K UAS by Jul 2026 under $1.4B total allocation. C-UAS demand now competing for same tactical budgets.
evidence_basis: JIATF-401 $600M+ commitment reported Apr 10 2026 (DefenseScoop). DDP congressional appropriation $1.4B for 200K+ UAS through 2027. Navy deploying Coyote interceptors on USS Bainbridge/Churchill/Levin (TWZ Apr 10). Hezbollah FPV escalation and Hormuz blockade drive urgent C-UAS spending.
probability_basis: Strong primary source on $600M JIATF-401 spend. Inferred crossover point based on C-UAS acceleration vs. stretched DDP timeline (30K by Jul vs 200K by 2027 suggests budget phasing).
drivers: Hezbollah FPV ramp + Iran Hormuz blockade create urgent C-UAS demand

co_occurrence_signal.pattern: 93 co-occurrences — C-UAS now dominant acquisition category, pulling budget from traditional UAS programs

event: Allied co-production demand creates Europe BOM divergence risk by H1 2027 — 26 Ukrainian defense firms seeking French co-production deals (DefenseNews Apr 10). France has 1 Ukraine JV vs Germany's 11, Spain's 5. European domestic content mandates will force BOM splits from US Blue UAS configurations.
evidence_basis: 26 Ukrainian firms in Paris seeking co-production (DefenseNews Apr 10). France 1 JV, Germany 11, Spain 5 per Ukrainian Council of Defence Industry CEO. European industrial policy drives local sourcing — diverges from US NDAA/Blue UAS supply chain requirements.
probability_basis: Verified diplomatic push and JV count disparity. Timeline inferred from typical 12-18mo co-production setup cycles. European content mandates create structural pressure for different component sources than US programs.
drivers: 26 Ukrainian firms seeking French co-production partnerships
Germany 11 JVs, Spain 5, France 1 — competitive industrial policy
European domestic content requirements vs US Blue UAS framework

lead_time_signal.component: European FPV BOM (flight controllers, ESCs, cameras)

event: Laser C-UAS systems create new FAA airspace restriction layer by Q3 2026 — Pentagon/FAA agreement for high-energy laser C-UAS along Mexico border (DefenseNews Apr 10) follows Feb 25 friendly-fire shootdown incident. Commercial aviation avoidance zones will constrain drone operations near borders, military bases.
evidence_basis: Pentagon/FAA signed agreement for laser C-UAS on southern border (DefenseNews Apr 10). FAA conducted safety testing in New Mexico after Feb 25 2026 laser shootdown of US government drone. Two prior incidents raised safety concerns.
probability_basis: Verified FAA agreement and testing. Timeline inferred from typical FAA Notice to Airmen (NOTAM) implementation cycles post-safety validation. Impact on commercial drone ops is second-order effect.
drivers: Pentagon/FAA high-energy laser C-UAS agreement signed Apr 10 2026
FAA safety testing in New Mexico validates airspace restrictions needed

co_occurrence_signal.pattern: 39 co-occurrences — C-UAS evolving from RF jamming to kinetic/directed energy, creates new regulatory constraints

event: F-16 Block 70 air-to-air becomes viable anti-drone platform — Bahrain scored first Block 70 air-to-air kills against Iranian drones (TWZ Apr 10). Export customers (Poland, Slovakia, Bulgaria, Iraq, Taiwan) now have proven C-UAS capability, may shift procurement from dedicated ground C-UAS to integrated air defense.
evidence_basis: Royal Bahraini Air Force F-16D Block 70 shot down 2 Iranian drones (TWZ Apr 10, confirmed via RBAF social media). First air-to-air kills by Block 70 variant. Multiple export customers operate or ordered Block 70s.
probability_basis: Verified shootdowns. Procurement impact is inferred — air forces may reprioritize integrated air defense over dedicated C-UAS if fighter-based intercepts prove cost-effective. Early signal, not certain trend.
drivers: Bahrain F-16 Block 70 confirmed 2 Iranian drone kills Apr 2026
Block 70 export base: Poland, Slovakia, Bulgaria, Iraq, Taiwan
Air-to-air intercept cheaper than dedicated C-UAS systems at scale

event: Space Force Andromeda contract creates parallel procurement model for autonomous swarms — $1.8B program awards to 14 vendors for RG-XX constellation (DefenseScoop Apr 10). Same autonomy + mesh + AI stack required for space domain awareness applies to terrestrial drone swarms. Dual-use technology acceleration.
evidence_basis: Space Force awarded 14 companies access to $1.8B Andromeda contract for next-gen space domain awareness (DefenseScoop Apr 10). RG-XX constellation first delivery. Autonomous coordination + sensor fusion identical to UAS swarm requirements.
probability_basis: Verified contract award. Technology crossover is inferred — space SDA uses same AI/autonomy/mesh tech as UAS swarms (Lattice, Hivemind architectures). Dual-use acceleration likely but not contractually confirmed.
drivers: Space Force $1.8B Andromeda contract to 14 vendors Apr 10 2026
RG-XX constellation autonomy + mesh tech identical to UAS swarm stack
DoD cross-domain technology transfer (space SDA → terrestrial C2)

co_occurrence_signal.pattern: 54 co-occurrences — AI-driven autonomy becoming universal across space, air, ground domains
